comment.body:
Now regarding the risk across the Taiwan Strait, my amateur guess is that China is not yet ready militarily. And strengthening Russia is indeed a very effective way to contain China. It is not too late. 

I also estimate that the risk of the EU switching to China in the near term is not very high, as 1. China would be making a U-turn directly confronting Russia this way, 2. the EU would also be making a U-turn allying with Russia’s main sponsor in the war… Even though as Alma wrote, the left in the EU and communist China are consistent in ideology.

Notably, today Ursula & co are meeting with leaders of Central Asia, a strategic location to both China and Russia.
comment.raw_body:
Now regarding the risk across the Taiwan Strait, my amateur guess is that China is not yet ready militarily. And strengthening Russia is indeed a very effective way to contain China. It is not too late. 

I also estimate that the risk of the EU switching to China in the near term is not very high, as 1. China would be making a U-turn directly confronting Russia this way, 2. the EU would also be making a U-turn allying with Russia’s main sponsor in the war… Even though as Alma wrote, the left in the EU and communist China are consistent in ideology.

Notably, today Ursula & co are meeting with leaders of Central Asia, a strategic location to both China and Russia.
